profile_overlap_rules.reason: Packet.decode cumulative is a subset of pyav_iter_frames cumulative
to_ndarray cumulative is a subset of pyav_iter_frames cumulative
all execute sequentially in the same thread

pipeline_execution_model.description: pyav_iter_frames is a Python generator. detect_changes consumes it in a synchronous for loop. Each iteration: (1) the generator yields the next sampled VideoFrame via builtins.next, (2) ROI + extract_features + visual_distance + threshold execute in the same thread, (3) the loop body completes before the next generator advance. All processing is single-threaded: no worker pool, no queue, no concurrent decode/extract. Two passes both execute this same pattern sequentially.

The gap is directionally stable across all three runs.

candidate_evaluation.wall_clock_evidence: Directly measured: pass1_decode_or_frame_advance 96.36347230006504s (35.35%) + pass2_decode_or_frame_advance 76.09364630002165s (27.92%) = 172.457s (63.27%). Total decode exceeds extract_features by 79.84s.

candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Total decode (172.46s, 63.27%) is the dominant wall-clock cost at nearly double the second-largest component. The gap (79.84s, 29.29%) is directionally stable across all three runs. F-0097 gap resolved: residual dropped from 87.39s (33.0%) to 0.53s (0.19%), confirming all decode work is captured by non-overlapping timers.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: The decode pipeline cost is split across two passes. Each individual pass decode time is comparable to extract_features (pass1_decode 96.36s vs extract_features 92.62s — gap 3.75s). However, the decode pipeline runs the SAME code twice. An optimization targeting decode reduces cost in both passes proportionally.
candidate_evaluation.ruling: PRIMARY — total decoder pipeline wall-clock SAFELY DISCRIMINATED as the dominant bottleneck. Evidence: (1) residual near zero confirming full attribution, (2) gap directionally stable across all runs, (3) profile corroboration of Packet.decode as largest single function.

confidence_rationale: Total decode pipeline wall-clock (172.46s, 63.27%) dominates all other work by a factor of ~1.86x over the next largest component (extract_features, 92.62s). The gap (79.84s) is directionally stable across all three runs. Residual has dropped from 87.39s (33.0%) to 0.53s (0.19%), confirming full attribution. The decode pipeline runs identical code (Packet.decode + generator advancement + to_ndarray) in both passes. Profile evidence corroborates Packet.decode (114.85s self) as the largest single function.

rejected_optimizations.name: PYAV_HWACCEL_CUDA
rejected_optimizations.rejection_reason: The hypothesized optimization claimed PyAV defaults to software H.264 decode and Step 18.5 should add hwaccel=cuda to av.open(). Source audit at the Step 18.4 candidate HEAD (acb424f) at which PYAV_HWACCEL_CUDA was evaluated proved the pyav_iter_frames at THAT HEAD already: (1) prefers 'cuda' first in _HW_PREFERRED, (2) calls _pick_hw_device(), (3) calls _create_hwaccel() to build a HWAccel object, (4) passes hwaccel=hwaccel to av.open(str(video_path), hwaccel=hwaccel). The hypothesis target already existed in source at that HEAD. NOTE: this is HISTORICAL source state at the rejection HEAD, preserved for traceability; it is NOT a current-source claim. The current production path calls _create_hwaccel_with_evidence() (see current_source_evidence). No committed benchmark evidence proves whether hardware decode was actually active at runtime (see F-0098).
false_source_assumptions: PyAV defaults to software H.264 decoder
Step 18.5 needs to add hwaccel=cuda to av.open() options dict
Existing benchmark decode is definitely software-only
rejected_optimizations.current_source_evidence: CURRENT production source (pyav_backend.py at the merged master HEAD da35cf34): _HW_PREFERRED = ['cuda', ...]; pyav_iter_frames calls _pick_hw_device(), then _create_hwaccel_with_evidence() (the evidence-capturing variant), and passes the resulting hwaccel to av.open(str(video_path), hwaccel=hwaccel). The plain _create_hwaccel() still exists but is NOT called by the production pyav_iter_frames path; it appears only in the HISTORICAL source description above (rejection-time HEAD acb424f).
rejected_optimizations.historical_context_preserved: This hypothesis was the initial Step 18.4 decision before source audit. It is preserved in git history and in bottleneck_decision.md under REJECTED_HYPOTHESIS.

optimization_candidate_evaluation.current_behavior: As of the Step 18.4A canonical evidence run (2026-07-14): private evidence observer exists and was exercised on the canonical Hermes H.264 clip. Schema separates codec_context_is_hwaccel (raw, observed=true) from actual_hardware_decode_active (UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN by design). _create_hwaccel_with_evidence captures creation errors (none observed). Deterministic generator cleanup. 17/17 tests pass. Canonical probe status OBSERVATION_COMPLETE; outcome OUTCOME_D.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.mechanistic_cost_targeted: Observability gap: closed. The evidence mechanism exists, is tested, and has now produced a canonical Hermes H.264 runtime evidence object. Observability does not change decode performance.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.supporting_evidence: 17/17 tests pass. Observer captures all required fields from production path. Canonical Hermes H.264 evidence collected (runtime_evidence_commit 9b4cc54). Probe validates provenance (--accepted-base, --canonical-mode).
optimization_candidate_evaluation.counter_evidence: N/A — observability does not change decode performance.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.missing_evidence: None for the observability purpose. (Direct HW-decode activation remains UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN — see F-0098 — but that is an activation-determination gap, not an observability gap.)
optimization_candidate_evaluation.step18_5_viability: Observability-only. Cannot be the sole optimization for Step 18.5.

optimization_candidate_evaluation.current_behavior: Canonical Hermes H.264 runtime evidence (2026-07-14): HWAccel requested=cuda, object created without error, container opened with hwaccel, raw codec_context.is_hwaccel=true, but actual_hardware_decode_active=UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN by observer design (actual-HW-decode conclusion confidence = NOT_ESTABLISHED). The strict fallback control was attempted but is INVALID (INVALID_SUPPORTING_CONTROL_IMPLEMENTATION_DEFECT, premature break) and provides no signal.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.mechanistic_cost_targeted: If runtime evidence proves CUDA was requested but not active or software fallback occurred, fix the activation/fallback defect to enable hardware decode.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.supporting_evidence: NONE — canonical Hermes H.264 production-path evidence does NOT directly prove hardware decode was inactive and does NOT directly prove a software fallback occurred (actual_hardware_decode_active = UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN by observer design). The strict fallback control is INVALID and provides no signal. No activation defect is directly proven.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.counter_evidence: N/A — no direct evidence either way (actual_hardware_decode_active = UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN).
optimization_candidate_evaluation.missing_evidence: F-0098 (PARTIALLY_RESOLVED): a direct proof that hardware decode is active or that a software fallback occurred. A corrected strict fallback control (demux until first frame / EOF / exception) is a candidate future experiment. The canonical evidence narrows but does not close this gap; without it, no bounded activation/fallback fix can be selected.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.quality_risks: Hardware decode may produce slightly different pixel values vs software decode.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.backend_compatibility_risks: Requires FFmpeg/PyAV built with CUDA/CUVID support.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.step18_5_viability: Not viable without F-0098 evidence first.

optimization_candidate_evaluation.current_source_boundary: detect_slides.run_detect_slides: Pass 1 and Pass 2 both call decoder.iter_frames() which triggers pyav_iter_frames, paying the full decoder/frame advancement pipeline twice (172.46s total).
optimization_candidate_evaluation.current_behavior: The same decoder/frame advancement pipeline is executed twice. Pass 1 samples 1201 frames for feature extraction and change detection. Pass 2 samples 1201 frames for representative timestamp matching and screenshot capture.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.mechanistic_cost_targeted: Eliminate the second full decode pass by reusing Pass 1 frame data or by decoding only the frames needed for Pass 2 representative timestamps.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.supporting_evidence: Total decoder/frame advancement pipeline = 172.46s (63.27%). Pass 2 accounts for 76.09s (27.92%) of total. A successful elimination would save the full pass2_decode_or_frame_advance time.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.counter_evidence: Representative timestamps become known only after Pass 1 completes (change detection determines segment boundaries). The second pass must re-decode to retrieve frames at specific representative timestamps. Memory cost of retaining all 1201 sampled 1920x1080 RGB frames: 1201 * 1920 * 1080 * 3 = ~7.0 GB. Current representative_frames = 84 (522547200 bytes). Retaining all 1201 frames is infeasible. An alternative design would need to cache decoded frames at sample timestamps during Pass 1 or use a frame index for targeted re-decode.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.missing_evidence: Exact memory vs time tradeoff analysis. Feasibility of caching 1201 frames or a subset. Whether segment representative timestamps always correspond to sampled frame timestamps.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.quality_risks: Frame reuse may produce different pixel values if decoder state differs between passes (e.g., HW vs SW decode).
optimization_candidate_evaluation.resource_risks: Memory: retaining all 1201 RGB frames = ~7 GB. Retaining 84 representative frames = ~0.5 GB (current post-dedup cost). Intermediate strategies possible.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.backend_compatibility_risks: Caching strategy must work for both OpenCV and PyAV backends.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.cancellation_progress_risks: Caching during Pass 1 increases per-frame memory and latency. Progress reporting unchanged.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.step18_5_viability: Requires detailed design analysis of memory constraints, representative timestamp semantics, and exact signature preservation. Not a bounded single intervention without prior analysis.

optimization_candidate_evaluation.current_source_boundary: pyav_backend.py lines 113-157: pyav_iter_frames generator. av.open(str(video_path), hwaccel=hwaccel) call, container.demux(stream), packet.decode() loop.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.current_behavior: Default PyAV/FFmpeg decoder configuration. No explicit codec, thread count, or decoder option overrides.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.mechanistic_cost_targeted: FFmpeg decoder configuration options (threads, flags, skip_frame, etc.) may reduce decode time without changing output pixels.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.supporting_evidence: NONE — no committed benchmark evidence measures decode time under alternative configurations.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.counter_evidence: Accepted benchmark evidence does not establish whether HW acceleration was active at runtime (see F-0098). No committed comparative decoder-configuration benchmark exists. Therefore the effect of thread count or other decoder options is unknown. No optimization-specific gain estimate is accepted.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.missing_evidence: Which FFmpeg decoder options are available via PyAV. Whether thread count, flags, or skip_frame are applicable to hardware decode paths. Baseline decode time under alternative configurations.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.quality_risks: Decoder option changes may alter output pixels (e.g., skip_frame may skip non-reference frames).
optimization_candidate_evaluation.resource_risks: Thread count increase may increase CPU usage without proportional decode speedup.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.backend_compatibility_risks: Options specific to PyAV/FFmpeg — not applicable to OpenCV backend.
optimization_candidate_evaluation.step18_5_viability: Undetermined without evidence. Not a bounded single intervention without knowing which option and expected effect.

step18_5_hypothesis_detail: Step 18.4 has selected DECODE_FRAME_PIPELINE with HIGH confidence. Canonical Hermes H.264 HWAccel runtime evidence has now been collected (Step 18.4A done, decision outcome OUTCOME_D). The production-path observations prove HWAccel was requested (cuda), the HWAccel object was created without error, the container was opened with HWAccel, and the raw codec_context.is_hwaccel is true; however actual hardware decode activation remains UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN by observer design (actual-HW-decode conclusion confidence = NOT_ESTABLISHED), and the strict fallback control is INVALID (INVALID_SUPPORTING_CONTROL_IMPLEMENTATION_DEFECT) and provides no signal. No targeted decoder optimization can be selected safely: PYAV_HWACCEL_CUDA is REJECTED_SOURCE_MODEL_MISMATCH; no activation defect is directly proven (so PYAV_HARDWARE_DECODE_ACTIVATION_OR_FALLBACK_FIX is not viable); and the remaining candidates are not bounded single interventions supported by committed evidence. Step 18.5 remains planned but blocked until Step 18.4 selects a targeted optimization.
evidence_gap_description: F-0097 RESOLVED (residual 87.39s/33.0% -> 0.53s/0.19%). Primary bottleneck DECODE_FRAME_PIPELINE SAFELY DISCRIMINATED. F-0098 PARTIALLY_RESOLVED: canonical Hermes H.264 runtime evidence object now exists and is complete (OBSERVATION_COMPLETE, canonical observation completeness confidence = HIGH) and codec_context_is_hwaccel=true is confirmed on the canonical clip; however actual_hardware_decode_active remains UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN by observer design (actual-HW-decode conclusion confidence = NOT_ESTABLISHED). The strict fallback control is INVALID (implementation defect, premature break) and provides no signal. F-0100 RESOLVED (canonical clip located and copied local-only, not committed).
f0097_link: F-0097 — RESOLVED by instrumentation: pass1_decode_or_frame_advance + pass2_decode_or_frame_advance + pass2_match_and_collect timers. Decoder/frame advancement pipeline wall-clock is captured.
f0098_link: F-0098 — PARTIALLY_RESOLVED. Canonical Hermes H.264 runtime evidence object collected (see canonical_hwaccel_runtime_evidence). Evidence mechanism corrected and tested (17/17); canonical observations complete (OBSERVATION_COMPLETE, observation_consistency=true); codec_context_is_hwaccel=true confirmed on the canonical clip; actual_hardware_decode_active remains UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN by observer design (actual-HW-decode conclusion confidence = NOT_ESTABLISHED; canonical observation completeness confidence = HIGH). The strict fallback control is INVALID (implementation defect) and provides no signal. No targeted optimization selected.

canonical_hwaccel_runtime_evidence.outcome_reason: Canonical H.264 runtime observations succeed (frames yielded in two independent production-path opens, observation_consistency=true, OBSERVATION_COMPLETE), but actual hardware decode state remains UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN. The UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN determination rests solely on a production-observer design limitation: the installed PyAV 18.0.0 API does not expose actual hardware decode vs configured HWAccel, so the observer reports UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN by design (this is NOT inferred from decode speed and NOT inferred from a missing hw_config). The strict fallback control was attempted but is INVALID (INVALID_SUPPORTING_CONTROL_IMPLEMENTATION_DEFECT — see strict_fallback_control.interpretation) and therefore carries no evidentiary weight either way. cuda WAS requested and available (not OUTCOME_C). Hardware decode activation confidence is NOT_ESTABLISHED; canonical observation completeness confidence is HIGH.

strict_fallback_control.interpretation: INVALID_SUPPORTING_CONTROL_IMPLEMENTATION_DEFECT — the strict-control loop executes an outer break immediately after the inner packet.decode() loop, so it surrenders after the FIRST demuxed packet regardless of whether a frame was produced. For this H.264 stream the first packet yields no frame, so the control reports STRICT_PROBE_NO_FRAME. This is an implementation defect of the supporting control, NOT a runtime discriminator signal. It carries NO evidentiary weight on actual HW decode activation or software fallback. The control must be corrected (continue demux until the first decoded frame, EOF, or an exception) before it can be used in any future HWAccel evidence decision. The canonical production-path observations (which DO yield frames) are unaffected and remain valid.

raw_artifact_known_limitation_omission.decision_layer_qualification: The raw artifact's evidence_confidence=HIGH refers ONLY to canonical observation completeness (two consistent production-path opens, OBSERVATION_COMPLETE). It is NOT a confidence statement about actual HW decode activation. The raw limitations=[] omits three by-design/non-determination facts that the decision layer must surface: (1) actual_hardware_decode_active=UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN by design (production observer does not expose actual HW decode vs configured HWAccel); (2) software_fallback_detected=UNKNOWN_NOT_PROVEN; (3) the strict fallback control result STRICT_PROBE_NO_FRAME is an INVALID_SUPPORTING_CONTROL_IMPLEMENTATION_DEFECT (premature break after the first packet), not a runtime signal. The raw artifact is a provenance object and is intentionally NOT rewritten.
